claim: ce_authority_ts is provably equal to ce_primary (both init to 1, both increment by 1 only in CEViewChange). Eliminating ce_authority_ts removes a redundant state variable, reducing state space and clarifying that under TEE trust the session number IS the monotonic authority counter.
proof_of_redundancy: Init: ce_authority_ts=1, ce_primary=1. Only mutation: CEViewChange sets ce_authority_ts'=ce_authority_ts+1 and ce_primary'=ce_primary+1. No other action modifies either. Therefore ce_authority_ts=ce_primary is an inductive invariant.
preserved_properties: INV6 PhantomLeaderRejection: vc.ts <= ce_primary (was ce_authority_ts, same value)
All session-based staleness checks unaffected (they already use ce_primary)
VC message ts field still carries the correct monotonic value

diff.description: Remove ce_authority_ts variable, replace all references with ce_primary

changes.reason: remove Init assignment (ce_primary = 1 already present at line 161)
remove from UNCHANGED clauses in CEProduceDirectEffect, CEProcessFill, CEBackupApply, CEBackupCrash, CECheckpoint, CEProcessRecoveredPending, OBProduceEffect, OBProducePendingAction, OBReceiveMFR
remove new_ts (equal to new_session), keep new_session only
VC message ts uses new_session (numerically identical to old new_ts)
INV6 uses ce_primary as the monotonic bound
INV17 downstream cache coherence uses ce_primary
INV17 downstream cache coherence uses ce_primary

state_space_impact: removes 1 Nat-valued variable from CE state. ceVars tuple shrinks from 10 to 9 elements. state space reduced by factor of MaxSeq (one fewer counter dimension).
risk: low. the redundancy proof is mechanically verifiable: ce_authority_ts = ce_primary is inductive. all downstream comparisons use the same numerical value. no semantic change to any reachable behavior.
